description: UCGIS 2026 workshop reference KG — Builder-level rules with executable validators

behaviors.name: Builder Behaviors Root
behaviors.description: Root of the builder-role behaviors tree.

behaviors.name: Every Skill Must Have a Script
behaviors.description: Every type=skill node has a non-empty path that resolves on disk.

behaviors.name: Every Behavior Declares an Enforcement Tier
Every Non-Soft Behavior Has a Validator
behaviors.description: Every behavior with enforcement != 'soft' has links.validator pointing to a real Python file.

behaviors.name: KG Node IDs Are Unique
behaviors.description: Globally across all 6 active KGs, no two nodes share an id.

behaviors.name: Catalog UI must tell the full story (8 visible sections)
behaviors.description: Every starter/<app>/index.html must contain all 8 sections marked with data-catalog-role attribute (title, provenance, acquisition, data-shape, processing, kg-link, reuse, data-view). A catalog missing any of these is a missing story; CI must fail until they are present.

behaviors.name: Catalog must embed Schema.org Dataset JSON-LD
behaviors.description: Every starter/<app>/index.html must contain a <script type=application/ld+json> block whose JSON parses, has @context=schema.org and @type=Dataset, and includes minimum required fields (name, description, url, license, creator, distribution, dateModified). This makes every workshop catalog automatically discoverable by Google Dataset Search and FAIR registries.

behaviors.name: Cline rules must match canonical system-prompt KG node
behaviors.description: Cline (.clinerules/01-builder-agent-prompt.md) is a generated mirror of the canonical system-prompt KG node at docs/builder/concepts/builder-agent-system-prompt.md. If the two drift apart, Cline operates on a different system prompt than the standalone agentloom.builder_agent (Layer-3 host #1) and Cursor (host #2), and the framework's host-agnostic claim collapses. The new Tier-A validator clinerules_must_match_system_prompt_kg_node.py delegates to sync_clinerules.py --check; this behavior node wires it into the canonical rule set.
